task_completion.description: Partition the floor plan to create at least two physically enclosed spaces using at least three new wall segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Sweet Home 3D 2D plan view, look for at least three newly drawn wall segments that form or contribute to at least two physically enclosed spaces (e.g., a Walk-In Cold Storage room, Driver Lobby).

task_completion.description: Define and name at least four room zones and apply distinct floor treatments to at least three of these zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for text labels in the 2D plan indicating at least four named room zones. Check the 3D view or 2D plan for distinct floor colors/textures applied to at least three of these rooms. (Note: Pre-existing rooms and floors count towards this total).

task_completion.description: Furnish the facility with at least 6 cooking or cooling appliances.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the 2D plan or 3D view for at least 6 appliances such as ovens, stoves, fryers, refrigerators, or freezers placed within the layout.

task_completion.description: Furnish the facility with at least 8 prep surfaces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the 2D plan or 3D view for at least 8 work tables, counters, or kitchen islands placed in the layout.

task_completion.description: Furnish the facility with at least 4 sanitation items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the 2D plan or 3D view for at least 4 sinks, washbasins, or dishwashers placed in the layout.

task_completion.description: Furnish the facility with at least 6 storage units.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the 2D plan or 3D view for at least 6 shelving units, racks, or cabinets placed in the layout.

task_completion.description: Add at least two dimension lines across main prep aisles to document clearance widths.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the 2D plan view for at least two dimension lines (lines with measurement numbers, typically with arrows at the ends) drawn across the empty spaces (aisles) between prep surfaces or walls.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open with evidence of mouse clicks, dragging furniture from the catalog, drawing walls, or using the dimension tool, rather than just terminal commands editing the .sh3d file.

integrity.description: Furniture and equipment are placed logically within the floor plan.
integrity.visual_evidence: Items should be distributed across the floor plan in a way that resembles a kitchen layout. They should not be dumped in a single overlapping pile just to bypass the item count requirements.

integrity.description: The agent saved the completed layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the agent clicking 'File > Save', using the save icon in the toolbar, or a terminal command confirming the application was closed after saving.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The starter file 'ghost_kitchen_starter.sh3d' is actually a stripped multi-room residential house containing 48 existing walls, 10 pre-existing named rooms (e.g., 'Bedroom #1', 'Bathroom'), and 8 colored floors. Due to a bug in the evaluation logic, the agent automatically passes the 'Zoning & Flooring' criteria (4 named rooms, 3 colored floors) because the baseline file already exceeds these requirements. Do not penalize the agent if it relies on these pre-existing rooms/floors to satisfy the zoning and flooring requirements; visually verify that the final state contains at least 4 named rooms and 3 colored floors, regardless of whether the agent created them.
